title: Full Workflow for Untargeted Metabolomics on Extracellular Vesicles - MetaboHUB Tours
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qc44","text":"The purpose of this workflow is to describe how unstructured data is processed using two approaches:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"difrt","text":"1) detection and annotation of features present in the data","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7lkn8","text":"2) identification of compounds in our files using FlashEntropy and a spectral database","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ft9t","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5ukk","text":"Each process will generate an identification table as output, which can be cross-referenced to annotate certain features (note the confidence level of the identification).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bg417","text":"If needed, all the software used has a GitHub repository or a dedicated website with training datasets and detailed tutorials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Data have been acquired on a Q-Exactive. For more details, please refer to the dedicated protocols : https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.eq2lyoq9qgx9/v1 Q-Exactive (Mass Spectrometer, ThermoScientific)
".raw" files are converted into ".mzML" files on MSconvert , with the filter "PeakPicking : MSlevels 1-2"
Bulding an In-House MS2 Spectral Library
Recovering Public Spectral Libraries
Identification
All standards compounds have been injected on the LC-MS system in LC-MS². Standards are coming from the MSMLS, OAMLS,BACSMLS and FAMLS chemical library from IROA Technologies (purchased from Merck).
Features detection and MS2 spectra extraction have been carried out on raw files, with Spec2Xtract . Output files are ".msp" ones
All these files have been concatened with FragHub ; leading to a unique library file in POS and in NEG.

Softwares and References : Spec2Xtract GitHub Sylvain Dechaumet FragHub 1.4.1 https://pubs.acs.org/doi/full/10.1021/acs.analchem.4c02219 FragHub: A Mass Spectral Library Data Integration Workflow.. Analytical chemistry.

GNPS libraries relate to clinical context have been downloaded : GNPS-NIH-CLINICALCOLLECTION1, GNPS-NIH-CLINICALCOLLECTION2, GNPS-NIH-SMALLMOLECULEPHARMACOLOGICALLYACTIVE, GNPS-COLLECTIONS-PESTICIDES-POSITIVE, GNPS-COLLECTIONS-PESTICIDES-NEGATIVE, GNPS-NIST14-MATCHES & MONA GNPS Mingxun Wang, Jeremy J Carver, Vanessa V Phelan, Laura M Sanchez, Neha Garg, Yao Peng, Don Duy Nguyen, Jeramie Watrous, Clifford

Softwares & References High resolution mass spectrometry for structural identification of metabolites in metabolomics. Metabolomics. Flash Entropy Search GitHub Yuanyue Li Flash entropy search to query all mass spectral libraries in real time.. Nature methods.

Identification of features from the QC is carried out in two stages, using Flash Entropy Only with the in-house msp files, generated in step 1.1 –> level of identification = 1 Only with the GNPS-based spectral libraries msp files, generated in step 1.2 –> level of identification = 2 or 3 These identification levels are based on :

All MS² spectra comparison and so, identification, is done manually with the parameters :Report top n hits = 5Precursor m/z tolerance (in Da) = 0.01Product ions m/z tolerance (in Da) = 0.02identity_search-score < 0.75 are not checked As an ouput files, you have a csv files with all hits found. Compounds with a identity_search-score < 0.75 are deleted. Identification are sorted based on hits and manual curation on MS² “experimental vs. theoritical” spectra comparison.

At this stage, you may have several .csv files (one per modality). You can now merge them and operate some redundancies sorting. In our case : Only the best modality is kept for 1 metabolite, based on RT > Dead Volume (0-1 min) and/or lower CV(metabolite) on QCs Analysis Validation: the analyses were validated by observing the distribution of QCs among the samples using Principal Component Analysis (PCA). For all PCAs, the data were log-transformed and underwent UV scaling normalization. --> Another control of the quality is done with a PCA displaying " blank vs. blank of extraction vs. QC" to check that our identification are not only noises. A single .csv files with unique identified compounds (and their level of identification) and annotated features, with their corrected intensity in each samples.

Identified Compounds IDs At least, ID from CHEBI, the SMILES and the InChiKey must be displayed. An identified compound must be properly indexed. For example: - In metabolomics, phenylalanine is different (at least biologically speaking) from L-phenylalanine, which is different from D-phenylalanine. - In lipidomics, identification is complex. For example, the formula PC(34:2) encompasses many different structures, each with distinct roles. Not all mass spectremeters allow for their differentiation (going further: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jlr.2024.100621 ) CHEBI Malik, A., Arsalan, M., Moreno, C., Mosquera, J., Félix, E., Kizilören, T., Muthukrishnan, V., Zdrazil, B., Leach, A. R., and O'
Add 800 µL of methanol in each dried pellets samples
mzML files are uploaded on W4M , as a collection Keep the file extension because it will be necessary for further steps

XCMS findChromPeaks (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3) Spectra Filters Filter on mz : 60-850 Extraction Method for Peak Detection CentWave 3 ppm 4,20 Advanced options 3 3,25 000 (based on 2D ions maps, visualized with BatMass) Mean of the peak's mz values Mexican hat -0.001 10 000 (based on 2D ions maps, visualized with BatMass) No Yes or No
XCMS findChromPeaks merger (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3)

XCMS groupChromPeaks (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3) Method to use for grouping Peak density 3-5 to define for each experiment to define for each experiment (can be based on "number of blanks +1") 0.001 Get the Peak List Yes or No 5 2 maxo YES
XCMSadjustRtime (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3) Peak group 0.85 1 loess

XCMS groupChromPeaks (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3) Method to use for grouping Peak density 1-3 to define for each experiment to define for each experiment (can be based on "number of blanks +1") 0.001 Get the Peak List Yes or No 5 2 maxo YES
XCMS fillChromPeaks (Galaxy Version 3.12.0+galaxy3) Advanced options not modified Peak List Yes 5 2 maxo Yes
CAMERA groupFWHM (Galaxy Version 0.1.0+camera1.48.0-galaxy1) Intensities valuex : maxo Polarity : POS or NEG
CAMERA findIsotopes (Galaxy Version 0.1.0+camera1.48.0-galaxy1) Charge : 2 Expected isotopes : 2 Intensities : maxo % of samples : 0.6-0.7
CAMERA findAdducts (Galaxy Version 0.1.0+camera1.48.0-galaxy1)* General error : 3-5 ppm Polarity : POS or NEG Convert s in min

Batch correction (Galaxy Version 3.0.0) loess ( you need at least 8 QC ! ) prevent it batch basic (a good start) This step involves “normalizing” the signal. However, if biological sample are different, for example, if there are more or fewer cells in pellets, “biological” normalization is an additional step that must be performed.

Intensity check (Galaxy Version 1.3.0) & Quality metrics (Galaxy Version 2.2.8+galaxy0) Using both tools in multiple steps will allows you to calculte and filter on: foldchange, between blank and QC linearity of signals between QC and diluted QC CV(pool) < 30 % CV(sample) < CV(pool) Remove peaks with a Q3 at 0 in samples
Between-table Correlation (Galaxy Version 1.0.0)
Analytical correlation filtration (Galaxy Version 2019-06-20) Correlation threshold :0.85-0.90 Yes Yes or no 0.005 Yes 0.1 highest intensity
Intensity check (Galaxy Version 1.3.0) Filter on ACorF_groups to remove "0" and "-"
A .tsv file where every line correspond to a unique feature

Softwares and References: MSconvert (ProteoWizard) Chambers, M.C., MacLean, B., Burke, R., Amode, D., Ruderman, D.L., Neumann, S., Gatto, L., Fischer, B., Pratt, B., Egertson, J., A cross-platform toolkit for mass spectrometry and proteomics.. Nature biotechnology. Workflow4Metabolomics 25.0 http://bioinformatics.oxfordjournals.org/content/early/2014/12/18/bioinformatics.btu813 Workflow4Metabolomics (W4M): A User-Friendly Metabolomics Platform for Analysis of Mass Spectrometry and Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Data.. Current protocols.
Agitate vigouruosly during 00:00:30
Centrifugation 15000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes
1- Transfer 700 µL in a first 96-wells plate --> for reversed-phase analysis 2- From the 1st plate, transfer 350 µL in a second plate --> for hilic analysis
1- Reversed-Phase Analysis
2- HILIC Analysis
Evaporate : 00:30:00 under N2 stream at 40 °C
Resuspend in 100 µL of water/acetonitrile (8:2)
Planar agitation : 00:15:00 , strength 7/10 Cover the plate with an aluminium fold
Centrifugation 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes
Transfer 85 µL of each sample in a new 96-wells plates
Pipette and mix 5 µL of each sample to create a QC sample, then divide it into several aliquots for injection quality control
Cover with an injection-friendly fold
Evaporate : 00:30:00 under N2 stream at 40 °C
Resuspend in 100 µL of acetonitrile/water (8:2)
Planar agitation : 00:15:00 , strength 7/10 Cover the plate with an aluminium fold
Centrifugation 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes
Transfer 85 µL of each sample in an insert for 1.5 mL glass vial
Pipette and mix 5 µL of each sample to create a QC sample, then divide it into several aliquots for injection quality control
Seal with an injection-friendly cap
